title: Verify @google/genai SDK video generation API
description:
CRITICAL: Before implementation, verify the exact SDK method signatures for video generation.

Gemini review flagged: The spec uses `client.models.generateVideos` but this may not exist. Need to verify:
1. Does `generateVideos` exist on GoogleGenAI client?
2. Is it `client.models.generateVideos()` or different path?
3. What is the exact response structure (URI vs inline data)?
4. How does operation polling work with this SDK?

Create a standalone test script that:
- Initializes the client with GEMINI_API_KEY
- Calls the video generation method with a simple prompt
- Logs the operation object structure
- Polls until completion
- Inspects the response format (URI vs base64)

Output: Document the verified API signatures and response format.
acceptance_criteria:
- Test script runs successfully with real API
- Method signature documented
- Response format (URI vs inline) confirmed
- Operation polling pattern verified

title: Implement MCP server skeleton with tool registration

   - veo_generate_video

4. Define Zod schemas for each tool's parameters

title: Add MCP progress notification support
description:
HIGH from Gemini review: Add progress notifications during 30-90s generation.

Implementation:
1. Use MCP logging capability for progress updates
2. Send periodic updates during polling loop
3. Include estimated time remaining if possible

```typescript
// During polling loop
server.sendLoggingMessage({
    level: "info", 
    data: `Video generation in progress... (${attempts * 10}s elapsed)`
});
```

Alternative if available:
- Use MCP progress tokens
- Send percentage-based updates

This significantly improves UX for long-running operations.
acceptance_criteria:
- Progress updates visible in Claude Code
- Updates every poll interval (10s)
- Completion notification sent

title: Implement veo_generate_video tool (text-to-video)
description:
Primary tool: Generate video from text prompt.

Parameters (with Zod schema):
- prompt: string (required) - Detailed video description
- model: enum (default: veo-3.1-generate-preview)
  - veo-3.1-generate-preview
  - veo-3.1-fast-generate-preview
  - veo-3.0-generate-001
  - veo-3.0-fast-generate-001
- aspectRatio: enum (default: 16:9) - "16:9" | "9:16"
- duration: enum (default: 8) - 4 | 6 | 8 seconds
- resolution: enum (default: 720p) - "720p" | "1080p"
- negativePrompt: string (optional) - Elements to exclude
- background: boolean (default: false) - Return immediately with operation ID

Implementation flow:
1. Validate parameters
2. Call generateVideos API (per VEO-1 findings)
3. If background=false: poll until complete, save video, return path
4. If background=true: return operationId for later check
5. Update session state
6. Send progress notifications during polling

Returns:
- path: string (file path to saved video)
- operationId: string
- duration: number
- metadata: { model, resolution, aspectRatio }
acceptance_criteria:
- Video generates from text prompt
- All parameter combinations work
- Video saves to ~/Documents/veo_generated/
- Background mode returns operation ID
- Progress notifications sent during generation

title: Implement veo_get_video_history tool
acceptance_criteria:
- Returns all generated videos
- Timestamps formatted correctly
- Empty array if no videos
- Sorted newest first

title: Implement veo_generate_transition tool (frame interpolation)
description:
Generate video transitioning between two frames.

Parameters:
- firstFrame: string (required) - Path to starting image
- lastFrame: string (required) - Path to ending image
- prompt: string (required) - Transition description
- duration: enum (optional) - Video duration
- model: enum (optional) - Model to use
- background: boolean (optional)

Implementation:
1. Validate both image paths exist and are supported formats
2. Read both images as base64
3. Use Veo 3.1 first/last frame feature
4. Model interpolates motion between frames

```typescript
const operation = await client.models.generateVideos({
    model: "veo-3.1-generate-preview", // Required for this feature
    prompt: prompt,
    firstFrame: {
        mimeType: getMimeType(firstFrame),
        data: firstFrameBase64
    },
    lastFrame: {
        mimeType: getMimeType(lastFrame),
        data: lastFrameBase64
    },
    config: { duration, ... }
});
```

Use cases:
- Day to night transitions
- Before/after morphs
- Character transformations
acceptance_criteria:
- Transition generates between two images
- Both images validated before API call
- Works with various image format combinations
- Clear error if frames incompatible

title: Implement veo_extend_video tool
acceptance_criteria:
- Extends Veo videos by 7 seconds
- Extension count tracked and limited
- Clear error for non-Veo videos
- Clear error at 20 extension limit

title: Implement veo_cleanup tool (storage management)
description:
From Gemini review: Video files are large (5-50MB+). Need cleanup tool.

Parameters:
- videoId: string (optional) - Specific video ID to delete
- all: boolean (optional) - Delete all session videos
- olderThan: string (optional) - Delete videos older than duration (e.g., "1h", "1d")

Implementation:
1. If videoId: delete specific video file and remove from history
2. If all: delete all videos in session and clear history
3. If olderThan: parse duration, delete matching videos
4. Return summary of deleted files and recovered space

```typescript
interface CleanupResult {
    deletedCount: number;
    bytesRecovered: number;
    deletedFiles: string[];
    errors?: string[];
}
```

Safety:
- Only delete files in OUTPUT_DIR
- Confirm file is in videoHistory before delete
- Handle already-deleted files gracefully
acceptance_criteria:
- Can delete specific video by ID
- Can delete all session videos
- Reports space recovered
- Safe: only deletes tracked videos

title: Implement comprehensive error handling
description:
Add robust error handling for all failure modes.

From spec + Gemini review:

1. Rate Limiting (429):
   - Implement exponential backoff
   - Clear message with retry suggestion

2. Content Policy Violation:
   - Catch policy blocks
   - Suggest prompt modification

3. Timeout:
   - Clear timeout message after 10 mins
   - Suggest using background mode

4. Invalid Input:
   - Validate image formats before API call
   - Validate video formats for extension

5. API Key Issues:
   - Clear message about missing key
   - Link to AI Studio

6. Network Errors:
   - Retry transient failures
   - Clear message for persistent failures

7. File System:
   - Permission denied (EACCES)
   - Disk full (ENOSPC)
   - Path not found

```typescript
function handleApiError(error: unknown): never {
    if (error instanceof ApiError) {
        switch (error.status) {
            case 401: throw new Error("Invalid API key. Check GEMINI_API_KEY.");
            case 429: throw new Error("Rate limited. Wait a moment and retry.");
            case 400: throw new Error(`Invalid request: ${error.message}`);
            // ... etc
        }
    }
    throw error;
}
```
acceptance_criteria:
- All error types have clear, actionable messages
- Rate limiting triggers backoff
- No raw errors exposed to user
- Errors logged to stderr with detail

Test structure:
```
tests/

    image-to-video.test.ts
    transitions.test.ts
    extend-video.test.ts

```

Note: These are slow tests (30-90s each). Run selectively.
acceptance_criteria:

- Tests are idempotent (clean up after)
- Can run individual tests
- Clear test output with timings

title: Integrated Skill → MCP Test Suite (nano-banana + veo)
description:
Test the full skill-guided creative workflow: Skills provide creative expertise, MCPs execute generation.

Architecture:
```
SKILL LAYER (Creative Brain)
├── nano-banana skill → image prompting expertise
└── veo3-prompter skill → video prompting expertise

MCP LAYER (Execution)
├── nano-banana MCP → Gemini image generation
└── veo MCP → Veo video generation
```

Test Scenarios:
1. nano-banana skill → nano-banana MCP (image generation)
2. veo3-prompter skill → veo MCP (video generation)
3. Full chain: image skill → image MCP → video skill → video MCP (image-to-video workflow)
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] nano-banana skill invokes correctly and guides prompt creation
- [ ] nano-banana MCP generates images from skill-guided prompts
- [ ] veo3-prompter skill invokes correctly and guides video prompts
- [ ] veo MCP generates videos from skill-guided prompts
- [ ] Full chain works: generate image → animate with video
- [ ] Document any bugs found and fix iteratively
notes:
## Pre-Test Fixes Complete (Dec 14, 2025)

### Bugs Fixed Before Testing:
1. **veo-mcp/src/index.ts** - Removed `generateAudio` parameter (not supported by Gemini API)
2. **veo3-prompter/SKILL.md** - Removed incorrect `generateAudio` documentation, added note about native audio generation
3. **~/.claude/skills/veo3-prompter/** - Synced updated SKILL.md

### MCP Server Rebuilt:
- `npm run build` completed in veo-mcp/
- Needs Claude Code restart to load new version

---

## BLOCKER: API Key Leaked (Dec 14, 2025)

**Error:** `Your API key was reported as leaked. Please use another API key.`

The GEMINI_API_KEY configured in MCP servers has been flagged by Google as compromised.

### Required Actions:
1. Go to [Google AI Studio](https://aistudio.google.com/)
2. Revoke the current API key
3. Generate a new API key
4. Update the key in Claude Code MCP configuration:
   - `claude mcp remove nano-banana`
   - `claude mcp add nano-banana --env GEMINI_API_KEY=NEW_KEY -- npx -y nanobanana-mcp`
   - Same for veo MCP server

### Resume After:
Once new API key is configured, resume Test 1.
